audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot priestly garment construction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for priestly garments, ephod, stones, breastpiece, robe, tunics, gold plate, completion formula, Moses' inspection and blessing.

verses.restored: From blue, purple, and scarlet they made woven garments for ministering in holy place, and made holy garments for Aaron, as YHWH commanded Moses.
He made ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen.
They hammered sheets of gold and cut threads to work into blue, purple, scarlet, and fine linen, work of skilled designer.
They made shoulder pieces for it, joined; at its two ends it was joined.
Skillfully woven band that was on it was from it, like its work: gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, as YHWH commanded Moses.
They made onyx stones set in gold filigree, engraved as signet engravings with names of sons of Israel.
He put them on shoulder pieces of ephod as stones of remembrance for children of Israel, as YHWH commanded Moses.
He made breastpiece, work of skilled designer, like work of ephod: gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen.
It was square; they made breastpiece doubled, a span its length and a span its width, doubled.
They filled it with four rows of stones: row of carnelian, topaz, emerald, first row;
second row: turquoise, sapphire, and diamond/clear stone;
third row: jacinth, agate, and amethyst;
fourth row: beryl, onyx, and jasper, set in gold filigree in their settings.
The stones were according to names of sons of Israel, twelve according to their names, engravings of signet, each according to its name for twelve tribes.
They made on breastpiece chains of corded work, twisted cords of pure gold.
They made two gold filigree settings and two gold rings, and put two rings on two ends of breastpiece.
They put two gold cords on two rings at ends of breastpiece.
Two ends of two cords they put on two settings, and put them on shoulder pieces of ephod, toward its front.
They made two gold rings and put them on two ends of breastpiece, on its edge that was toward inner side of ephod.
They made two gold rings and put them on two shoulder pieces of ephod below, toward its front, close to its joining, above woven band of ephod.
They bound breastpiece by its rings to rings of ephod with blue cord, so it was above woven band of ephod and breastpiece did not move from ephod, as YHWH commanded Moses.
He made robe of ephod, work of weaver, all blue.
Opening of robe in its middle was like opening of coat of mail, with binding around opening, so it would not tear.
They made on hem of robe pomegranates of blue, purple, and scarlet, twisted.
They made bells of pure gold and put bells among pomegranates on hem of robe all around, among pomegranates:
bell and pomegranate, bell and pomegranate, around hem of robe for ministering, as YHWH commanded Moses.
They made tunics of fine linen, work of weaver, for Aaron and his sons,
and turban of fine linen, beautiful caps of fine linen, linen undergarments of fine twisted linen,
and sash of fine twisted linen, blue, purple, and scarlet, work of embroiderer, as YHWH commanded Moses.
They made plate of holy crown of pure gold and wrote upon it writing of signet engraving: Holy to YHWH.
They put on it blue cord, to put it on turban above, as YHWH commanded Moses.
All work of dwelling-place of tent of meeting was completed; children of Israel did according to all that YHWH commanded Moses, so they did.
They brought dwelling-place to Moses: tent and all its vessels, clasps, frames, bars, pillars, and bases;
covering of reddened ram skins, covering of durable hides, and veil of screen;
ark of testimony, its poles, and cover;
table, all its vessels, and bread of presence/faces;
pure lampstand, its lamps, lamps arranged, all its vessels, and oil for light;
gold altar, anointing oil, fragrant incense, and screen of entrance of tent;
bronze altar, its bronze grating, poles, and all its vessels, basin and its stand;
hangings of court, its pillars and bases, screen for gate of court, its cords and pegs, and all vessels for service of dwelling-place, for tent of meeting;
woven garments for ministering in holy place, holy garments for Aaron the priest, and garments of his sons to serve as priests.
According to all that YHWH commanded Moses, so children of Israel did all the work.
Moses saw all the work, and look, they had done it; as YHWH commanded, so they had done. And Moses blessed them.

translation_notes: Repeated 'as YHWH commanded Moses' emphasizes faithful completion of the pattern.
Priestly garments again carry Israel's names for remembrance and heart-bearing.
Gold threads woven into fabric show metal transformed into textile artistry.
Moses inspects and blesses completed work; sacred craft receives blessing, not exploitation.
safety_note: Completion of sacred work leads to blessing, not further extraction. The artisans and congregation are honored for doing as commanded.
